purpose: Provides an explicit probability-weighted model of the correlation distribution for portfolio construction, showing why unconditional correlation estimates are misleading
components: Critical insight: distribution is bimodal, invalidating mean-variance optimization with the unconditional estimate
current_assessment: More actionable for portfolio construction than conjunction triggers. Unconditional near-zero correlation is robust to ±0.15 perturbations in regime weights. Regime probabilities are calibrated judgment rather than model output. Portfolio optimization using the unconditional correlation would be suboptimal.
limitations: Regime probabilities are calibrated judgment, not derived from a formal model
Two-regime assumption may be oversimplified (transitional subregimes exist)
Does not specify transition dynamics between regimes
Weights may not be stable over the 2-3 year forecast horizon
